questions.question: Who must be present to initially set up the Box connector in Notion AI?

options.text: A Box Admin and a Notion workspace owner
options.explanation: Both roles are required: the Box Admin can grant Box‑side permissions and the Notion workspace owner can authorize the integration.

options.text: Any Box user who can log into Notion
options.explanation: Regular Box users lack the administrative rights needed to grant the integration access.

options.text: Only a Notion workspace owner
options.explanation: A Notion owner alone cannot grant Box‑side access; a Box Admin is also required.

options.text: Any Notion admin on a free plan
options.explanation: The integration also requires a paid Enterprise Notion plan, not just admin rights.

questions.question: After disconnecting Box from Notion AI, what happens to the Box content in Notion searches?

options.text: The content stays searchable forever
options.explanation: Disconnecting removes the search index; the content is no longer searchable.

options.text: The content becomes immediately unsearchable
options.explanation: Notion follows a data‑minimization principle: once disconnected, Box files are instantly removed from the search index.

options.text: The files are deleted from Box automatically
options.explanation: Notion never deletes files from Box; it only stops indexing them.

options.text: The files are copied into Notion for offline access
options.explanation: Notion stores only embeddings, not the original files, and disconnecting removes those embeddings.

questions.question: How long does it typically take for new Box files to become searchable in Notion AI?

options.text: Instantly
options.explanation: There is a short processing window; new files are not searchable the moment they appear.

options.text: Within an hour
options.explanation: The connector checks for new content hourly, making the files searchable within that time frame.

options.text: After 24 hours
options.explanation: The hourly sync means the wait is much shorter than a full day.

options.text: Up to 72 hours
options.explanation: 72 hours refers to the initial integration setup, not to the indexing of new files.

questions.question: Which statement accurately describes how Notion AI handles Box permissions?

options.text: Notion AI ignores Box permissions and can read every file in the connected account.
options.explanation: Notion AI strictly follows the existing Box permission hierarchy.

options.text: Box permissions are inherited downward from parent folders, and Notion AI respects that inheritance.
options.explanation: Permissions flow from parent to child folders, and Notion AI mirrors this behavior.

options.text: Shared link permissions are fully respected by Notion AI.
options.explanation: Shared links are an overlay that can expand access, but the connector does not support them.

options.text: Users can access any file they can see in Box, even if they lack explicit permission on that file.
options.explanation: Access is limited to files the user is explicitly allowed to view, per Box’s permission model.

questions.question: A user has access only to "Folder 3" in Box. Which of the following files can they retrieve via Notion AI?

options.text: text.txt located in Folder 1
options.explanation: Access to Folder 1 is not granted, so its contents remain out of reach.

options.text: text.txt located in Folder 2
options.explanation: Folder 2 is higher in the hierarchy; without explicit permission, the user cannot see it.

options.text: text.txt located in Folder 3
options.explanation: Permissions are inherited downward, so anything inside Folder 3 is accessible to the user.

options.text: All files in the Box account
options.explanation: Permissions are not global; the user can only see what they are allowed to.

questions.question: When the Box‑Notion integration is first set up, how far back does it scan for existing files and folders?

options.text: Only files created in the last 30 days
options.explanation: The scan window is longer than a month.

options.text: All files ever created in the Box account
options.explanation: The connector limits the initial scan to a one‑year window.

options.text: Files and folders created, viewed, or modified in the past year
options.explanation: The integration looks back one year for any activity to populate the index.

options.text: Only files that have been explicitly shared with the Notion workspace
options.explanation: The scan is based on activity, not on explicit sharing.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid way to start the Box connection flow inside Notion?

options.text: Click "Connect Box" in the Notion AI panel at the bottom of the screen
options.explanation: This is the primary entry point described in the article.

options.text: Navigate to Settings → Notion AI, then click the "+" under Box
options.explanation: This is an alternative path provided in the documentation.

options.text: Open the Box admin console and enable the Notion AI integration there
options.explanation: The Box admin console is not used for this integration; setup occurs within Notion.

options.text: After installation, click the gear icon ⚙️ next to Box in the Notion AI Settings modal
options.explanation: The gear icon is used for managing or disconnecting, not for initial connection.

questions.question: What type of storage does Notion use for the content it indexes from Box?

options.text: A relational SQL database
options.explanation: Notion stores embeddings, which are not suited to relational tables.

options.text: A vector database such as Turbopuffer
options.explanation: Embeddings are stored in a vector database to enable fast similarity searches.

options.text: A traditional NoSQL document store
options.explanation: While NoSQL can hold documents, the article specifically mentions a vector DB.

options.text: A blockchain ledger for immutable storage
options.explanation: Blockchain is not used for this purpose.

questions.question: How does Notion AI treat Box shared‑link permissions?

options.text: Shared links expand access and Notion AI fully respects the expanded permissions.
options.explanation: Although shared links can broaden access in Box, the connector does not support them.

options.text: Shared links are an overlay that can expand access, but Notion AI currently ignores them.
options.explanation: The article states shared‑link permissions are not supported, so they are not considered by Notion AI.

options.text: Shared links are treated the same as normal folder permissions by Notion AI.
options.explanation: Shared links are a separate overlay; they are not mapped to permission checks.

options.text: Notion AI automatically revokes any shared‑link access once the integration is enabled.
options.explanation: No revocation occurs; the connector simply does not evaluate shared‑link scopes.

questions.question: If a Box collaboration expires at 3 PM, when will Notion AI stop providing responses that involve those files?

options.text: Immediately at 3 PM
options.explanation: Permission checks are performed on an hourly schedule, not instantly.

options.text: At the next hourly permission‑check cycle after 3 PM
options.explanation: The system checks expiration and retention rules hourly, so the change takes effect at the next check.

options.text: Exactly 24 hours after the expiration time
options.explanation: The article specifies hourly checks, not a daily delay.

options.text: Only after the next full synchronization of the Box integration (up to 72 hours later)
options.explanation: Expiration handling is independent of the initial 72‑hour integration window.
